_comment: Warhammer franchise data file. Covers Warhammer Fantasy, Warhammer 40,000, Age of Sigmar, and Black Library media.
description: Games Workshop's grimdark fantasy and sci-fi empire. Spans Warhammer Fantasy Battles (1983–2015), Warhammer 40,000 (1987–present), and Age of Sigmar (2015–present). Known for its intricate lore, Gothic aesthetic, and 'grimdark' tone. Massive media presence across video games, novels, animation, and comics.

media.title: Warhammer Fantasy Roleplay
media.description: TTRPG set in the Old World. 4th Edition published by Cubicle 7.
media.notes: Foundational media piece.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader (1st Ed. Rulebook)
media.description: The original 40K rulebook. More RPG than wargame, establishing the core lore of the setting.
media.notes: 1st Edition of Warhammer 40,000. Treat as foundational media.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Inquisitor (Skirmish Game)
media.description: Skirmish-level wargame focused on Inquisitorial retinues. 54mm scale.
media.notes: Out of print. Precursor to Inquisitor: Martyr video game.

media.title: Space Hulk (1993)
media.description: Turn-based strategy. Terminators vs Genestealers inside a Space Hulk.
media.notes: Multiple re-releases. 2013 remake by Full Control, Tactics (2018) by Cyanide.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Final Liberation
media.description: Epic-scale turn-based strategy. Imperial Guard vs Orks on Armageddon. Features FMV cutscenes.
media.notes: Based on the Epic 40,000 ruleset.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate
media.description: Turn-based strategy. Grey Knights vs Chaos Space Marines. Spiritual ancestor of Daemonhunters.
media.notes: Considered a classic of the era. Abandonware.

media.title: Warhammer: Shadow of the Horned Rat
media.description: Real-time tactical strategy set in Warhammer Fantasy. Brutal difficulty.
media.notes: Abandonware. Followed by Dark Omen (1998).

media.title: Warhammer: Dark Omen
media.description: Improved sequel to Shadow of the Horned Rat. Empire mercenary band vs Undead and Chaos.
media.notes: Abandonware. Cult classic among Fantasy fans.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Fire Warrior
media.description: First-person shooter. Play as a Tau Fire Warrior fighting Imperials, Orks, and Chaos.
media.notes: Mixed reception. PS2 version has a unique soundtrack.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War
media.description: Seminal RTS that revitalized the 40K license. Squad-based combat across 4 base races.
media.notes: Includes Winter Assault (2005), Dark Crusade (2006), Soulstorm (2008) expansions.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War II
media.description: Shifted from RTS base-building to tactical squad RPG. Blood Ravens vs Tyranids and Chaos.
media.notes: Includes Chaos Rising (2010) and Retribution (2011) expansions.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War III
media.description: Attempted to merge DoW I and II mechanics with MOBA elements. Mixed reception.
media.notes: Final entry in the Dawn of War series. Planned expansions cancelled.

media.title: Warhammer: Mark of Chaos
media.description: RTS set in Warhammer Fantasy. Empire vs Chaos. Battle March expansion adds Orcs and Dark Elves.
media.notes: GOG. Best pure Fantasy RTS.

media.title: Warhammer Online: Age of Reckoning
media.description: MMORPG set during the End Times. RvR (Realm vs Realm) PvP focused. Empire vs Chaos and more.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Official servers shut down 2013. Private server Return of Reckoning still active.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine
media.description: Third-person action game. Captain Titus of the Ultramarines fights Orks and Chaos on Forge World Graia.
media.notes: Cult classic. Backwards compatible on Xbox Series X.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine II
media.description: Direct sequel. Captain Titus returns to face a Tyranid invasion and Chaos corruption.
media.notes: Released September 2024. Massive commercial success — over 5 million copies sold.

media.title: Space Hulk (2013)
media.description: Digital faithful recreation of the 1989 board game. Turn-based, Blood Angels vs Genestealers.
media.notes: Available on Steam.

media.title: Space Hulk: Tactics
media.description: Card-based twist on Space Hulk mechanics. Two campaigns: Blood Angels and Genestealer Cults.
media.notes: Available on Steam.

media.title: Space Hulk: Deathwing
media.description: First-person shooter inside a Space Hulk. Dark Angels Deathwing Terminators vs Genestealers.
media.notes: Enhanced Edition (2018) improved significantly from launch.

media.title: Blood Bowl 2
media.description: Digital adaptation of the Fantasy American Football board game. Humans vs various Warhammer Fantasy races.
media.notes: Blood Bowl 3 (2023) is the current entry.

media.title: Blood Bowl 3
media.description: Latest entry in the digital Blood Bowl series. 12 races at launch, expanded via DLC.
media.notes: Launched with some controversy over monetization.

media.title: Mordheim: City of the Damned
media.description: Tactical RPG based on the skirmish board game. Warbands fight in the ruined city of Mordheim.
media.notes: Mixed reception but niche audience enjoyed it.

media.title: Necromunda: Underhive Wars
media.description: Turn-based tactical strategy in the underhive of Necromunda. Gang warfare.
media.notes: Same developer as Mordheim.

media.title: Necromunda: Hired Gun
media.description: Fast-paced FPS in the Underhive. Bounty hunter gameplay with grappling hook and cyber-mastiff.
media.notes: Movement-focused. Rough launch, patched significantly.

media.title: Battlefleet Gothic: Armada
media.description: Real-time strategy space battles in the Gothic Sector. Imperium vs Chaos, Orks, Eldar.
media.notes: Set during the Gothic War.

media.title: Battlefleet Gothic: Armada II
media.description: Larger scale sequel. 12 factions including Necrons, Tyranids, Tau. Gathering Storm campaign.
media.notes: Definitive 40K space combat game.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Inquisitor – Martyr
media.description: Diablo-style ARPG. Play as a Caligari Sector Inquisitor purging heretics, xenos, and daemons.
media.notes: Prophecy expansion adds Tech-Adept class. Ongoing seasonal content.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus
media.description: Turn-based tactical RPG. Adeptus Mechanicus Tech-Priests explore a Necron tomb world.
media.notes: Renowned for its soundtrack and atmosphere. Hereticus DLC adds a new campaign.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Gladius – Relics of War
media.description: 4X strategy game on a single planet. Space Marines, Orks, Necrons, Astra Militarum fight over ancient relics.
media.notes: Expansions added Chaos, Adeptus Mechanicus, Tyranids, and Tau. Base game free on Epic.

media.title: Total War: Warhammer
media.description: Total War strategy meets Warhammer Fantasy. Empire, Dwarfs, Greenskins, Vampire Counts in the Old World.
media.notes: First game in the trilogy. Standalone, but combined with later games via Immortal Empires.

media.title: Total War: Warhammer II
media.description: Expanded to Lustria, Ulthuan, and Naggaroth. High Elves, Dark Elves, Lizardmen, Skaven.
media.notes: Mortal Empires combined campaign. Widely considered the peak of the trilogy.

media.title: Total War: Warhammer III
media.description: Final entry. Set in the Chaos Realms. Kislev, Grand Cathay, and all four Chaos Gods factions.
media.notes: Immortal Empires unites all three games into one massive campaign.

media.title: Warhammer: End Times – Vermintide
media.description: Left 4 Dead-style co-op set during the End Times. The Ubersreik Five fight endless Skaven hordes.
media.notes: Set in the final days of Warhammer Fantasy.

media.title: Warhammer: Vermintide 2
media.description: Co-op melee horde shooter. Adds Chaos Warriors to the Skaven threat. Expanded career system.
media.notes: Still receiving updates as of 2026. Winds of Magic expansion adds Beastmen.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Darktide
media.description: Spiritual successor to Vermintide set in 40K. Rejects fight cultists and Chaos in hive city Tertium.
media.notes: PC Nov 2022, Xbox Oct 2023. PS5 version released 2024.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Battlesector
media.description: Turn-based strategy. Blood Angels vs Tyranids during the Devastation of Baal.
media.notes: Expansions added Necrons, Sororitas, Chaos Daemons, and more factions.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate – Daemonhunters
media.description: XCOM-style turn-based tactics. Grey Knights vs Nurgle's Bloom. Spiritual successor to 1998 Chaos Gate.
media.notes: Highly praised. Duty Eternal and Execution Force expansions.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Shootas, Blood & Teef
media.description: 2D side-scrolling run-and-gun. Play as an Ork Warboss reclaiming a stolen Squig. Metal soundtrack.
media.notes: Up to 4-player co-op.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader
media.description: CRPG by the Pathfinder developers. Explore the Koronus Expanse as a Rogue Trader — noble, explorer, and warrant-holder beyond the Imperium's edge.
media.notes: Released Dec 2023. Void Shadows DLC (2024) adds Drukhari content.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Boltgun
media.description: Retro boomer shooter in the vein of Doom and Quake. Sternguard Veteran clears a Forge World of Chaos.
media.notes: Highly praised. Forges of Corruption DLC adds new chapters.

media.title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Storm Ground
media.description: Turn-based strategy in the Mortal Realms. Stormcast, Nighthaunt, and Maggotkin factions. Roguelite campaign.
media.notes: First dedicated AoS video game.

media.title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Realms of Ruin
media.description: RTS with cinematic campaign. Stormcast Eternals vs Orruk Kruleboyz vs Nighthaunt.
media.notes: Released Nov 2023. Underperformed commercially but has a dedicated playerbase.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Freeblade
media.description: On-rails shooter. Pilot an Imperial Knight Freeblade defending the Imperium.
media.notes: Active as of 2026. Gacha progression.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Wolf
media.description: Turn-based card game RPG. Space Wolves vs Chaos on an ice world.
media.notes: Still available on Steam and mobile.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Combat Cards
media.description: Mobile digital CCG. Collect and upgrade units from across 40K factions.
media.notes: Active as of 2026.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Tacticus
media.description: Turn-based tactical mobile RPG. Collectible characters across multiple 40K factions.
media.notes: Active as of 2026. Cross-promoted with tabletop releases.

media.title: Warhammer: Chaos & Conquest
media.description: Base-building MMO strategy. Serve Chaos Gods to dominate the Old World.
media.notes: Active. Freemium base-builder — predatory monetization.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Freebooter
media.description: Defunct mobile RPG. Ork Freebooterz searching for loot.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Removed from stores. No longer playable.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Lost Crusade
media.description: Defunct MMO strategy. Build your Chapter and fight across the galaxy.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Removed from stores.

media.title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Realm War
media.description: Defunct mobile MOBA-lite. Stormcast vs Chaos in the Mortal Realms.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Servers closed 2021.

media.title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Champions
media.description: Digital TCG with physical card tie-ins. Servers closed 2022.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: No longer playable.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dark Nexus Arena
media.description: Cancelled MOBA. Never left Steam Early Access.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Cancelled during Early Access. Servers offline.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Eternal Crusade
media.description: Online third-person shooter. Four factions (Space Marines, Chaos, Orks, Eldar) fight over a planet.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Servers shut down 2020.

media.title: Dark Millennium Online
media.description: Cancelled 40K MMO. Shown at E3 2011, cancelled during THQ's bankruptcy.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Never released. Only trailers survive.

media.title: Warhammer: Odyssey
media.description: Mobile MMORPG set in Warhammer Fantasy. Limited soft-launch only.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Never widely released. Servers closed.

media.title: Warhammer: Vermintide – The End Times (Mobile)
media.description: Mobile companion app / mini-game for Vermintide. Lore collection and mini-game.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Removed from stores.

media.title: Astartes
media.description: Fan-made animated short series. Retributor Chapter Space Marines board a traitor vessel. ~12 min total. Officially adopted by Games Workshop.
media.notes: Originally YouTube. Now Warhammer+ exclusive. Defining piece of fan animation.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Angels of Death
media.description: Official animated series. Blood Angels Chapter fights Tyranids during the Cryptus Campaign. Rotoscope-animated.
media.notes: Warhammer+ exclusive. Voice acting from the gaming community.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Hammer and Bolter
media.description: Official animated anthology. Each episode explores a different faction or event across 40K and Age of Sigmar.
media.notes: Warhammer+ exclusive. Standout episodes: Death's Hand, Old Bale Eye, Bound for Greatness.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Pariah Nexus
media.description: CG animated series. Ultramarines, Sisters of Battle, and Grey Knights trapped in the Necron Pariah Nexus.
media.notes: Warhammer+ exclusive. Visually the most polished official animation to date.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: The Exodite
media.description: CG series. Tau Stealth Team infiltrates a world under Imperial and Chaos influence.
media.notes: Warhammer+ exclusive.

media.title: Helsreach
media.description: Fan-made animated adaptation of Aaron Dembski-Bowden's novel. Black Templars defend Hive Helsreach against Orks during the Third War for Armageddon. ~2 hr total runtime.
media.notes: YouTube. Officially acknowledged by Games Workshop.

media.title: Ultramarines: A Warhammer 40,000 Movie
media.description: Direct-to-DVD CGI film. Ultramarines investigate a distress signal on a Chaos-corrupted world.
media.notes: Mixed reception. Cast: John Hurt, Terence Stamp, Sean Pertwee. Only official 40K feature for over a decade.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Iron Within
media.description: Warhammer+ animated standalone special. Iron Warriors Chaos Marines face a planetary uprising. ~40 min.
media.notes: Warhammer+ exclusive.

media.title: Secret Level (Warhammer 40,000 Episode)
media.description: Standalone 40K episode in Amazon's Love, Death & Robots-style anthology series. Directed by Dave Wilson.
media.notes: Amazon Prime Video, Dec 2024. Henry Cavill executive producer.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: The Motion Picture (Cancelled)
media.description: Cancelled CGI film by Fanderson/SAB. First attempt at a 40K feature. Only trailer and BTS footage survive.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Cancelled. Lost media with surviving footage.

media.title: Eisenhorn (TV Series, Cancelled)
media.description: Cancelled live-action adaptation of Dan Abnett's Eisenhorn novels. Was in development at BBC.
media.notes: DEFUNCT: Cancelled. Only concept art exists.

media.title: The Horus Heresy Audio Dramas
media.description: Full-cast audio dramas set during the Horus Heresy. Titles include: Garro, Raven's Flight, The Burden of Loyalty, and many more.
media.notes: Produced by Black Library. Available on Audible and Black Library's site.

media.title: Eisenhorn: Xenos (Audio Drama)
media.description: Full-cast audio drama adaptation of Dan Abnett's Xenos. Inquisitor Eisenhorn investigates a heretical text.
media.notes: Available on Audible.

media.title: The Horus Heresy (Novel Series)
media.author: Various (Dan Abnett, Graham McNeill, Aaron Dembski-Bowden, et al.)
media.description: The foundational 60+ novel series detailing the civil war that shattered the Imperium. Begins with Horus Rising (2006), concludes with The End and the Death Vol. III (2024).
media.notes: The defining literary work of 40K. Essential reading for deep lore.

media.title: Eisenhorn
media.author: Dan Abnett
media.description: Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn trilogy: Xenos, Malleus, Hereticus. Cornerstone 40K literature.
media.notes: Best entry point for 40K fiction. Followed by Ravenor and Bequin trilogies.

media.title: Gaunt's Ghosts
media.author: Dan Abnett
media.description: Long-running series following the Tanith First-and-Only Imperial Guard regiment across the Sabbat Worlds crusade. 'Sharpe in space.'
media.notes: 16+ novels as of 2026. First novel: First and Only (1999).

media.title: Ciaphas Cain
media.author: Sandy Mitchell
media.description: Comedic series. Memoirs of a self-proclaimed cowardly Commissar. 10+ novels. Tone deliberately contrasts grimdark.
media.notes: Great entry point for readers who want lighter 40K fiction.

media.title: Night Lords Trilogy
media.author: Aaron Dembski-Bowden
media.description: Soul Hunter, Blood Reaver, Void Stalker. Perspective-shifting Chaos Space Marine story following the Night Lords Eighth Claw.
media.notes: Critically acclaimed. Challenges the reader's sympathies. Best Chaos fiction.

media.title: Gotrek & Felix
media.author: William King, Nathan Long, et al.
media.description: Long-running Warhammer Fantasy series. Slayer Gotrek and poet Felix Jaeger travel the Old World seeking Gotrek's prophesied doom.
media.notes: First novel: Trollslayer (1999). Continues in Age of Sigmar. Foundational Fantasy fiction.

media.title: The End Times
media.author: Various
media.description: Five-part series chronicling the destruction of the Warhammer Fantasy world and the birth of Age of Sigmar.
media.notes: Published 2014–2015. Controversial among fans but narratively complete.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000 Codex Series
media.author: Games Workshop
media.description: Core rulebooks and faction codices. Primary lore source beyond the novels. Each faction has extensive background.
media.notes: 10th Edition released 2023. Current editions available from Games Workshop.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Damnation Crusade
media.description: Graphic novel by Boom! Studios. Follows a Black Templar initiate from recruitment to death.
media.notes: Out of print. Predecessor to Titan Comics' current license.

media.title: Marneus Calgar
media.description: Marvel Comics 5-issue miniseries. Origin story of the Ultramarines Chapter Master. Written by Kieron Gillen.
media.notes: Best starting comic — written by a seasoned industry veteran. Collected edition available.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Will of Iron
media.description: Titan Comics series. Dark Angels investigate a mystery with ties to the Fallen. 4 volumes.
media.notes: First major Titan Comics run. Available in collected editions.

media.title: Warhammer 40,000: Sisters of Battle
media.description: Marvel Comics 5-issue miniseries. Novitiate Sister Protagonist faces crisis of faith on a contested planet.
media.notes: Good entry point for Adepta Sororitas lore.

media.title: Blood Bowl: More Guts, More Glory!
media.description: Titan Comics Blood Bowl series. Comedic tone consistent with the tabletop game. Follows the Skullcrushers team.
media.notes: Multiple volumes. Tie-in to the Blood Bowl tabletop revival.

media.title: Inferno! Magazine
media.description: Games Workshop / Black Library anthology fiction magazine. Contains short fiction, illustrated stories, and sequential art panels across all settings. Original run 1997–2003, relaunched 2018.
media.notes: Not strict comics format but includes visual narrative work. Relaunched issues available from Black Library.
